item.content:encoded: …Is a dick. Can we all agree?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article is great, from its incredibly unique concept, its execution, and its spot-on clinical tone. Transforming mental illnesses into something new, imaginative, and also something consistent with the entry’s theme is a good feat (a lot of other entries do this too, but still pulling it off deserves positive acknowledgement).
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not every lizard is 682. Here's a list of every page involving a reptile in some way.

item.title: Re: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Thank you Zyn, this puts more perspective on "necro posting" which is good to have, ill keep it in mind

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Dr Kramer, please don't backseat mod. Posting replies to old comments is totally fine if the new comment furthers the conversation in a meaningful way, and the previous comment is not related to a discussion that has run its course (for example, an old critique thread for a draft that is no longer being actively worked on).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Big oof. So glad I found this in the featured section…really hits me, I relate to this guy

item.title: Re: Another Idea.
item.content:encoded: Try not to post on comments that are two years old.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very interesting SCP, had me feeling sorry for this guy by the end, good job!
+1

item.title: Another Idea.
item.content:encoded: I Think That the original SCP should be 3052, and then all the 3052-2s should all be given a 3052-X Thats an idea. But I love this SCP too, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It really is, the Drug Overdose Part could hit home for some people, which makes this even better.

item.title: Oh god
item.content:encoded:
I think I'm going to cry ;-;

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A great addition. +1 #freestickman

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel bad for stick man. He's just a victim of his own creation. +1 #freestickman

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This resonates with me on a pretty personal level. I love how simple and clean, yet bittersweet it feels. Also, is the hateful, reptilian -2 meant to be an allusion to 682, or just a coincidence?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Severely underrated article. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Slight discrepancy. In the description, you say that the SCP is percieved as facing them by anyone viewing him/it, but in the video log the SCP is described as facing the West Wall. Does the effect not extend to videos of the SCP? If so, then couldn't they take accurate measurements by using video? Otherwise I enjoyed this one. I like the SCP's that aren't all "Murder Murder", but can still creep me out. This one creeps me out not because of the SCP itself necessarily, but because of what it seeing the -1 and -2 would. Just seeing this entity with nothing but two black eye holes staring at you being berated by a lizard, telling it/him to kill it/himself…. Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-3052-1's anomalous effects manifest when it is photographed or video recorded

Because being a literal walking, talking stick figure isn't anomalous already

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Took me a second read to piece together that last conversation between -1 and -2 and how it related to the digital animator, and that instantly elevated this to one of my favorites series IV entries. Among all the vivid horror that currently populates the 3000s, this stands out as an article that's melancholy in its depiction of the skip itself, but strangely heartwarming in how the Foundation treats it.

+1 with gusto.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is fucking incredible. Hate repeating what others have said- but the last log is just so fantastically executed and gives you such a feeling of emptiness. It'd be criminal for me to not +1 this thing. Keep doing what you're doing, OP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
poor little cartoon guy came to life and scared his creator to death :c

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually derived the most enjoyment from the way the Foundation handles this anomaly. They know it's potentially unstable, and aren't going out of their way to fuck with it.

Solid first work, author.

item.title: Revelations
item.content:encoded:
Had some pretty interesting revelations while thinking about this skip at work today.

1. -1 has no mouth and no bloodstream, so medication is virtually impossible.
2. -1 thinks the voices it hears are just that, voices in its head that no one else can hear. The Foundation knows the truth. The Foundation knows that -1's voices are more than just a result of a fractured psyche. They know that the voices are alive, intelligent, and want nothing more than to have -1 killed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Distinctly unsettling and resonant. An odd way of getting at the real core of the individual's surrender to their situational conditions, but an effective one nonetheless. Mechanically, the article's clean, and your clinical tone is spot on - especially important in this case, as necessitated by the fact that you're trying to describe both a person that needs care and the fact that they're essentially a messy jumble of organic components. The dialogue doesn't read as alien or cardboard, and the little sliver of exposition we get from the reptilian feels neither forced or stingy; rather, I believe the mild lack of context that I had elevated my enjoyment of the piece.

Excellent work. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was thinking we didn't need a second animate stick figure (I'm thinking of the guy made out of words), but that last log sold me. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Lots of SCP pages are really scary, lots are funny, and lots are just plain weird. This one, meanwhile, plays 'sad' and 'bittersweet' pretty much perfectly. Enthusiastically +1'd!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For someone relatively new here, you are far ahead of those who try to do something and end up failing miserably (without wanting to mock anyone here).

And also, I like interesting ideas written well: a sad pivot full of sweetness, very original, because I have not seen any resemblance to this SCP, that is, I read an original "work".

+1'd

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
an interesting idea, I always enjoy such things

And the story in the end, referring to the animator as "God" due to him creating them, and then he dies of drug overdose because they talked to him…

Amazing!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I originally did that in earlier drafts, except 3052-2 was 3052-A. Someone suggested that I change it around. I dunno, now that I think about it, maybe your idea is better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A wonderful mixture of sad and sweet, not to mention a great representation of depression. I felt sorry for 3052-1 by the end, the poor thing, but at least it has its little potted plant to take care of.

Why refer to it as -1 and the hallucinations as -2, though? Why not refer to the stick figure as 3052 and the hallucinations as -1? I dunno, I guess that's a technical nitpick, but otherwise I really dig this. +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Makes me think of The Wall. That's a good thing.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's finally up!

Thanks to Zachary Maxwell, mlister, and MeguminTDC for the feedback! (Wish I knew how to link you guys ;~;)
